question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getStreamType() always returns StreamType.RECORD (hardcoded, no runtime check) to cause a type confusion when the CutoverService is in BLOCK mode, where RecordDownloaderProperties is still consulted for scheduling decisions, potentially causing the record downloader to run when it should be suppressed by the cutover logic?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot Actuator /actuator/beans endpoint (if exposed) to discover the bean name recordDownloaderProperties and then use /actuator/env to target it specifically, with precondition that Actuator endpoints are exposed without auth, to disable only the record downloader while leaving balance downloaders active, causing selective data staleness?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user set frequency to Duration.ofMillis(1) via /actuator/env, with precondition that Actuator is exposed, causing the @Scheduled(fixedDelayString = '#{@recordDownloaderProperties.getFrequency().toMillis()}') in RecordFileDownloader to schedule downloads every 1ms, overwhelming the cloud storage provider (S3/GCP) with requests and triggering rate limiting or account suspension?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: RecordDownloaderProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Component('recordDownloaderProperties') bean name by registering a competing bean definition through a classpath manipulation attack (e.g., placing a malicious JAR with a same-named component in the classpath), with precondition of classpath write access, causing Spring to use the attacker's bean instead of the legitimate RecordDownloaderProperties, overriding all download configuration?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the writeFiles feature by enabling it via /actuator/env and then triggering a high-volume download scenario, with precondition that Actuator is exposed and the importer is processing a backlog of record files, causing the filesystem to fill up and crash the JVM with a java.io.IOException: No space left on device, leading to complete service unavailability?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that enabled=true is the default and there is no re-enable mechanism once setEnabled(false) is called by Downloader.verifySignatures(), meaning that if an attacker can trigger the endDate condition (e.g., by manipulating the endDate property via Actuator), the downloader is permanently disabled until restart, causing a persistent denial of service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot relaxed binding rules for Duration properties (which accept formats like '500', '500ms', 'PT0.5S') to inject an ambiguous or malformed duration string via environment variable, with precondition of environment variable injection capability, causing a ConversionFailedException at startup that prevents the importer from starting?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between persistBytes=true and the database transaction size limits, with precondition that persistBytes is enabled via Actuator, causing large record files (up to 50 MiB) to be included in database transactions, potentially exceeding PostgreSQL's max_wal_size or causing transaction timeouts defined in recordParserProperties.getTransactionTimeout(), leading to repeated transaction rollbacks and data loss?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the writeFiles=true path combined with a symlink attack on the archive destination folder, with precondition that the attacker has local filesystem access and writeFiles is enabled, causing Utility.archiveFile() to follow a symlink and overwrite arbitrary files on the filesystem, leading to data corruption or privilege escalation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the /actuator/env GET endpoint (if exposed) to read the current value of hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.record.enabled, with precondition that Actuator is exposed without auth, to determine whether the mirror node is actively ingesting record files and use this information to time attacks when the node is in a degraded state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the frequency field controls the fixedDelay (not fixedRate) of the scheduler, meaning the delay is measured from the end of the last execution, by setting frequency to 0ms via /actuator/env, with precondition that Actuator is exposed, causing back-to-back download invocations with no delay between them, exhausting connection pool resources in CommonDownloaderProperties.sources?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: RecordDownloaderProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @Validated annotation's dependency on the Spring validation framework by crafting a property value that passes @NotNull validation but causes a downstream failure in Duration parsing (e.g., a string that is non-null but not a valid ISO-8601 duration), with precondition of property injection capability, causing an unhandled exception during bean initialization?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the writeSignatures feature combined with a high number of consensus nodes, with precondition that writeSignatures is enabled via Actuator and the network has many nodes, causing Downloader.verifySignatures() to iterate all signatures and write each to disk, creating O(n) disk writes per record file where n is the number of consensus nodes, amplifying disk I/O to cause storage exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared CommonDownloaderProperties reference returned by getCommon() to read sensitive cloud storage credentials (accessKey, secretKey) via /actuator/configprops, with precondition that Actuator is exposed without auth, leaking cloud storage credentials that could be used to access or modify the record stream bucket?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Spring Boot Actuator /actuator/env POST endpoint to set hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.record.enabled=false followed by /actuator/restart to apply the change, with precondition that Actuator restart endpoint is enabled, causing a controlled restart that applies the disabled state and prevents record ingestion from resuming?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the persistBytes flag interaction with the StreamFile.setBytes(null) call in Downloader.verifySignatures() (which only nulls bytes when isPersistBytes() is false), by enabling persistBytes via Actuator, causing raw record file bytes to be retained in memory throughout the entire processing pipeline, leading to heap memory exhaustion (OOM) when processing large batches?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the SpEL expression #{@recordDownloaderProperties.getFrequency().toMillis()} used in @Scheduled by injecting a frequency value that causes toMillis() to overflow (e.g., Duration.ofDays(Long.MAX_VALUE / 86400 + 1)), with precondition of property injection capability, causing an ArithmeticException or Long.MAX_VALUE overflow that breaks the scheduler?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the writeFiles feature by enabling it via /actuator/env and then observing the archive destination folder structure (leaked via error logs or /actuator/logfile), with precondition that Actuator is exposed, to infer the internal network topology and node account IDs of the Hedera consensus nodes being monitored?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: RecordDownloaderProperties()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @RequiredArgsConstructor injection of CommonDownloaderProperties to cause a circular dependency or bean initialization failure by manipulating the Spring application context, with precondition of classpath manipulation, causing the RecordDownloaderProperties bean to fail initialization and preventing the entire importer from starting?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the combination of writeFiles=true and a high-frequency polling interval (500ms default) to create a sustained disk write attack, with precondition that writeFiles is enabled via Actuator, writing record files to disk at the rate of one per 500ms, and since record files can be up to 50 MiB, potentially writing up to 100 MiB/second to disk until storage is exhausted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/record/RecordDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the enabled flag is a plain boolean (not volatile or AtomicBoolean) in a multi-threaded Spring Boot application, causing a Java memory model visibility issue where the JIT compiler caches the value of enabled in a CPU register, and a thread that calls setEnabled(false) does not immediately propagate the change to threads reading isEnabled(), allowing downloads to continue after being disabled?
